step_by_step_thinking:
Agreeing with my prior conclusions aligns best here due to several reasons. Firstly, local resistance patterns guide optimal antibiotic selections ensuring better therapeutic success while minimizing resistance development—a key principle highlighted previously. Secondly, broad spectrum agents carry inherent drawbacks beyond just increased costs; specifically heightened risks of nosocomial infections and overall poorer long-term public health implications due to fostering bacterial resistance. Lastly, though empiric treatment might offer some benefit initially, it lacks specificity needed especially amidst evolving pathogen dynamics observed across different regions globally.


Final
